title: Reactive System
description: CReact tracks dependencies at the signal level. Changes propagate synchronously through a DAG of signals and computations.
signals_body: A signal holds a value and maintains a list of observers (computations that depend on it). When the value changes, all observers are marked stale and scheduled for re-execution.
computations_body: A computation is a function that reads signals. Any signal read during execution registers the computation as an observer. The dependency graph builds at runtime.
ownership_body: Every computation has an owner (the computation or root that created it). This forms a tree. When an owner is disposed, all its children are disposed too, including their effects and cleanup functions.
step_schedule_body: stale computations are added to the update queue
step_batch_body: multiple writes in a synchronous block are batched
step_run_queue_body: pure computations (memos) run first, then effects
step_clean_body: before a computation re-runs, its old dependencies are cleaned
module_tracking_desc: Core scheduler: runUpdates, runQueue, runComputation, cleanComputation
module_effect_desc: createEffect, createComputed, createRenderEffect, createReaction, onCleanup, onMount
glitch_free_body: The system guarantees that no computation observes an inconsistent state. Memos (pure computations) run before effects, and dependencies are resolved upstream-first. This eliminates "glitches" where a computation sees partially-updated values.
callout_synchronous: The reactive system is entirely synchronous within a batch. Async operations (like <0>useAsyncOutput</0> handlers) are managed by the runtime layer above.
